metadata.name: Information Delivery Process
metadata.description: Lifecycle-oriented information delivery workflow in the ISO 19650 draft, emphasizing information purposes, requirements, production planning, and exchange to support decisions.
metadata.author: ISO19650 Knowledge Maps

nodes.label: Lifecycle Decision
nodes.description: Decision taken during the lifecycle of an asset which requires supporting information.

nodes.label: Information Purpose
nodes.description: Reason why information is required to support a lifecycle decision.

nodes.label: Information Requirement
nodes.description: Specification of information needed to fulfill an information purpose.

nodes.label: Information Production Requirements
nodes.description: Information requirements relating to an appointment. (DEPRECATED: Exchange Information Requirements)

nodes.label: Information Production Planning
nodes.description: Planning how information will be produced by information production teams.

nodes.label: Information Production Plan
nodes.description: Document explaining the overall approach that an information production team will take. (DEPRECATED: BIM Execution Plan)

nodes.label: Information Production Schedule
nodes.description: Details of information containers to be produced during an appointment. (DEPRECATED: task information delivery plan, master information delivery plan)

nodes.label: Information Production
nodes.description: Creation and coordination of information to meet defined requirements, including generation, checking, review, approval, authorization and acceptance.

nodes.label: Information Checking
nodes.description: Quality assurance check of information containers against the information production standard.

nodes.label: Review and Approve
nodes.description: Technical review of information container by appointed party and approval for a specified use.

nodes.label: Review and Authorize
nodes.description: Review of information model by lead appointed party and authorization of its approved use.

nodes.label: Review and Accept
nodes.description: Review of information model by appointing party and acceptance of its approved use.

nodes.label: Information Exchange
nodes.description: Transfer of verified information between stakeholders.

nodes.label: Information Container
nodes.description: Unit of information delivered and managed within the common data environment.

nodes.label: Information Model
nodes.description: Structured collection of information containers used to support decisions.

nodes.label: Common Data Environment
nodes.description: Shared information environment used to manage and exchange information.

nodes.label: Information Production Milestone
nodes.description: Defined milestone where information must be delivered. (DEPRECATED: information delivery milestone)

evidence.quote: Information is required to support decision-making during the lifecycle of assets.
Information is only specified and produced in response to one or more clear information purposes.

Information production requirements are defined by the appointing party and can also be defined by a lead appointed party. Information production requirements should be expressed in such a way that they can be incorporated into appointments.

Planning for information production is the responsibility of each lead appointed party and their appointed parties after information production requirements and information constraints have been established and notified to them.

A range of resources should be developed during this process to describe information production in the necessary detail... an information production plan to explain the overall approach that an information production team is going to take.

A range of resources should be developed during this process to describe information production in the necessary detail... an information production schedule to show detailed timings of production of information containers by each appointed party in an information production team.
Information production is planned and carried out by each information production team.
Information production includes generation, coordination, checking, reviewing, approval, authorization, acceptance and aggregation of information containers.
Each appointed party shall undertake a quality assurance check of each information container... Once the check is complete, each appointed party shall... review and approve information container.
The lead appointed party shall... review and authorize the information model.
The appointing party shall undertake a review of the information model... and accept the information container(s).
Once accepted, the appointing party aggregates the information models into the asset information model(s).
Information exchanges deliver verified information containers between parties.
Information models are collections of information containers.
The common data environment is the agreed source of information containers.
Information models support decision-making throughout the lifecycle of assets.
Information production milestones define the events or dates when information is required by the organization(s) receiving it.
The appointing party shall establish the information production milestones to be met by the selected lead appointed party during the appointment.
